title: Cynoglossum capusii
intro: Cynoglossum capusii, also known as Chinese hound's tongue, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Cynoglossum capusii contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory conditions.

Cynoglossum capusii is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). 
It is native to the Himalayas and is also found in other parts of Asia. 
The classification of Cynoglossum capusii is as follows: genus - Cynoglossum, family - Boraginaceae, order - Borales, class - Magnoliopsida, and kingdom - Plantae. 
This plant is often referred to as Himalayan hare's ear due to its distinctive ear-shaped leaves.
uses: Cynoglossum capusii, commonly known as the Chinese hound's tongue, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. In addition, Cynoglossum capusii is thought to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections and skin conditions. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its purported ability to regulate bowel movements. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
